title: best herbal teas for anxiety
intro: Herbal teas for Anxiety are a type of herbal remedy that uses various plant-based ingredients to alleviate symptoms of anxiety and promote relaxation. These teas work by interacting with the body's natural chemistry, reducing stress and anxiety levels, and promoting a sense of calm. Examples of herbal teas used to treat anxiety include chamomile, which has a soothing effect and calms the mind, lavender, which has a calming effect and reduces stress, peppermint, which has a calming effect and reduces anxiety, passionflower, which has a sedative effect and calms the nervous system, and valerian root, which has a sedative effect and promotes relaxation. Additionally, other herbal teas such as lemon balm, hawthorn, and skullcap are also used to treat anxiety due to their calming and sedative properties, and can help to reduce symptoms of anxiety and promote a sense of well-being.

teas.plant_desc: Valeriana officinalis tea contains bioactive constituents such as valepotriates, isovaleric acid, and valerenic acid that help to treat anxiety by interacting with the brain's GABA receptors, promoting relaxation and reducing stress. The properties of this herbal tea include sedative, anxiolytic, and antispasmodic effects that help to calm the mind and body, reducing symptoms of anxiety and promoting a restful sleep. To make this tea, steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried valerian root in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-10 minutes, then strain and let it cool before consumption. To treat anxiety, consume 1-2 cups of valerian tea 30 minutes to 1 hour before bedtime to promote relaxation and improve sleep quality.

Lavandula angustifolia tea contains bioactive constituents such as linalool and linalyl acetate, which have been shown to have anxiolytic properties. The calming and soothing effects of this herbal tea are attributed to its sedative and relaxing properties, which help to reduce anxiety and promote a sense of calmness. To make this tea, steep 1 teaspoon of dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes, then strain and drink 1-2 cups per day. To consume this tea for anxiety relief, drink it 30 minutes to 1 hour before bedtime or whenever you feel anxious, and combine it with deep breathing exercises or meditation for enhanced relaxation effects.

Avena sativa tea contains bioactive constituents such as avenacosides, avenalol, and ferulic acid that help to treat anxiety by promoting relaxation and reducing stress levels. The calming properties of this herbal tea, including its sedative and anxiolytic effects, make it an effective remedy for alleviating anxiety symptoms. To make this tea, steep 1 teaspoon of dried Avena sativa leaves in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes, then strain and let it cool. To consume this tea, drink it 2-3 times a day, ideally before bedtime, to promote a restful and anxiety-free sleep.

Zingiber officinale tea contains bioactive constituents such as gingerols and shogaols, which have been shown to have anxiolytic properties, helping to alleviate symptoms of anxiety. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of this tea also contribute to its anxiolytic effects, promoting relaxation and reducing stress levels. To make Zingiber officinale tea, steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried ginger root in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes, then strain and enjoy. Consuming 1-2 cups of this tea per day, ideally before bedtime, can help to calm the mind and body, promoting a restful night's sleep and reducing anxiety symptoms.

Camellia sinensis tea contains bioactive constituents such as L-theanine, caffeine, and polyphenols that help to alleviate anxiety symptoms by promoting relaxation and reducing stress levels. The anxiolytic properties of this tea are attributed to its ability to regulate the body's response to stress, balance the nervous system, and enhance mood. To make camellia sinensis tea, steep one teaspoon of loose-leaf tea in a cup of boiling water for 3-5 minutes, then strain and enjoy. Consuming one to two cups of camellia sinensis tea per day, either hot or iced, can help to reduce anxiety and promote a sense of calmness and well-being.

Passiflora incarnata tea contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which help to alleviate anxiety by interacting with the neurotransmitters and hormones in the brain. The anti-anxiety properties of this tea are attributed to its ability to modulate the activity of GABA receptors, serotonin, and dopamine, promoting relaxation and reducing stress. To make this tea, steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried Passiflora incarnata flowers in a cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes, then strain and drink as needed. To treat anxiety, consume 1-2 cups of Passiflora incarnata tea per day, preferably 30 minutes to 1 hour before bedtime, to promote a restful night's sleep and alleviate symptoms of anxiety.

Melissa officinalis tea contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, triterpenoids, and rosmarinic acid that contribute to its anxiolytic properties, helping to alleviate anxiety symptoms. This herbal tea has a calming effect on the nervous system due to its sedative and adaptogenic properties, which enable it to balance the body's response to stress. To make Melissa officinalis tea, steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried Melissa officinalis leaves in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes, then strain and let it cool. To consume the tea, drink 1-2 cups per day, ideally before bedtime, to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety.

Silybum marianum tea contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which have been found to have anxiolytic properties that help to reduce anxiety. The anti-anxiety properties of this tea are attributed to its ability to modulate the activity of neurotransmitters, promoting relaxation and reducing stress. To make this tea, steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried Silybum marianum leaves in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes, then strain and drink 2-3 times a day. Consume this tea in moderation, as it is recommended to start with small amounts and gradually increase the dosage as needed, while also considering it as a complementary treatment to seek professional help for anxiety.

Matricaria chamomilla tea contains bioactive constituents such as apigenin, luteolin, and chamazulene, which are known to possess anxiolytic and calming properties that help to alleviate anxiety symptoms. The tea's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties also contribute to its anxiety-relieving effects by reducing stress and promoting relaxation. To make this tea, simply steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried Matricaria chamomilla flowers in a cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes, then strain and let it cool. To consume, drink 1-2 cups of the tea per day, ideally before bedtime, to promote a restful night's sleep and reduce anxiety.

Ginkgo biloba tea contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids and terpenoids, which are known to have a positive effect on anxiety by promoting relaxation and reducing stress levels. The properties of this herbal tea, including its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, help to calm the mind and body, reducing feelings of anxiety and promoting a sense of well-being. To make this tea, simply steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried ginkgo biloba leaves in a cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes, then strain and enjoy. Consuming 1-2 cups of ginkgo biloba tea per day can help to alleviate anxiety symptoms, and it is recommended to drink it in the morning or before bedtime to promote a restful night's sleep.

Passiflora edulis tea contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides that help to treat anxiety by promoting relaxation and reducing stress levels. The tea's properties, including its sedative and anxiolytic effects, help to calm the mind and body, reducing symptoms of anxiety and promoting a sense of well-being. To make this tea, combine 1 teaspoon of dried Passiflora edulis leaves with 1 cup of boiling water, let it steep for 5-7 minutes, and strain before consumption. Consuming 1-2 cups of this tea per day can help to alleviate anxiety symptoms and promote a peaceful state of mind.

Humulus lupulus tea contains bioactive constituents such as xanthohumol and isohumulones, which have been shown to possess anxiolytic and anti-inflammatory properties. The tea's properties, including its sedative and adaptogenic effects, help to alleviate anxiety symptoms by promoting relaxation and reducing stress levels. To make this tea, steep 1 teaspoon of dried Humulus lupulus flowers in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes, then strain and discard the solids. To consume this tea for anxiety relief, drink 1-2 cups per day, ideally before bedtime or during periods of high stress.

Scutellaria baicalensis tea contains bioactive constituents such as baicalein and baicalin, which have been shown to possess anti-anxiety properties. This herbal tea has sedative and calming effects due to its ability to reduce stress hormones and promote relaxation, making it an effective natural remedy for anxiety. To make Scutellaria baicalensis tea, simply steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried roots in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes, then strain and let cool. Consuming this tea 2-3 times a day, either hot or cold, can help to alleviate anxiety symptoms and promote a sense of calm and well-being.

Tilia platyphyllos tea contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are known for their anxiolytic and sedative properties, helping to alleviate anxiety symptoms by reducing stress and promoting relaxation. The properties of this herbal tea, including its calming effects and ability to regulate the nervous system, contribute to its effectiveness in treating anxiety. To make Tilia platyphyllos tea, steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried flowers in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes, then strain and let cool. To consume this tea, drink it 2-3 times a day, ideally before bedtime, to help regulate sleep patterns and reduce anxiety levels.

Glycyrrhiza glabra tea contains bioactive constituents such as glycyrrhizin, flavonoids, and phenolic acids that have been shown to possess anxiolytic properties, helping to alleviate anxiety symptoms. This herbal tea exhibits adaptogenic properties, which enable it to help the body adapt to stress, thereby reducing anxiety levels. To make this tea, combine 1 teaspoon of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root with 1 cup of boiling water, let it steep for 5-7 minutes, and then strain the liquid. To consume this tea for anxiety relief, drink 1-2 cups per day, ideally before bedtime, to promote relaxation and improve sleep quality.

Citrus reticulata tea contains bioactive constituents such as limonoids, flavonoids, and essential oils that help to treat anxiety by promoting relaxation and reducing stress levels. This herbal tea also possesses adaptogenic properties, allowing it to help the body adapt to stress and promote a sense of balance and calmness. To make this tea, combine dried citrus reticulata peel with boiling water, let it steep for 5-7 minutes, and then strain the liquid to create a soothing brew. To treat anxiety, consume 1-2 cups of citrus reticulata tea per day, ideally before bedtime to promote a restful night's sleep and alleviate morning anxiety.

Vitex agnus-castus tea contains bioactive constituents such as iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have been found to have anxiolytic and sedative properties. The tea's ability to regulate the body's hormonal balance, particularly estrogen levels, helps to alleviate symptoms of anxiety. To make this tea, steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried Vitex agnus-castus leaves in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-10 minutes, then strain and consume. Drinking 1-2 cups of this tea per day, ideally before bedtime, can help promote relaxation and reduce anxiety symptoms.

Schisandra chinensis tea contains bioactive constituents such as schisandrins, schisandrin B, and gomisin A, which have been shown to have anxiolytic and adaptogenic properties, helping to reduce stress and anxiety levels. The tea's adaptogenic properties allow it to help the body adapt to stressful situations, promoting balance and stability. To make this tea, steep 5-7 grams of dried Schisandra chinensis berries in 1 liter of boiling water for 10-15 minutes, then strain and drink as needed. Consuming 1-2 cups of this tea per day can help alleviate anxiety symptoms, and it is recommended to consume it in the morning or before bedtime to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety.

Hypericum perforatum tea contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and naphthodianthrones, particularly hyperforin and amentoflavone, which are responsible for its anxiolytic and adaptogenic properties. These constituents help to regulate the body's response to stress, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety levels. To make this tea, steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried Hypericum perforatum flowers in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-10 minutes, then strain and drink. Consuming this tea 2-3 times a day can help alleviate anxiety symptoms, and it is recommended to drink it in moderation, ideally before bedtime, to promote a restful sleep and reduce anxiety.

Cannabis sativa tea contains a variety of bioactive constituents, including cannabidiol (CBD), tetrahydrocannabinol (THC), and flavonoids, which have been shown to have anxiolytic properties. The herbal tea's adaptogenic properties help to regulate the body's response to stress, promoting a sense of calm and relaxation. To make this tea, steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried cannabis sativa leaves in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes, then strain and enjoy. Consuming this tea 1-2 times a day, ideally before bedtime, can help to alleviate anxiety symptoms and promote a restful night's sleep.
